destinations.evidence: Both node executor construction paths call the worker availability check and convert failures into startup errors.
The worker check resolves RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN or the adjacent binary, verifies existence, warms the pool, and errors when missing.

equivalence_argument: Node startup checks for ritual-jq-worker in nodebuilder before constructing the EVM/executor, so a missing worker aborts the node instead of deferring to runtime precompile calls.
notes: Same implementation evidence as invariant 541, with the narrower single-path invariant wording.
rationale: The merge adds ensure_jq_worker_available to executor setup and implements that check as a system-wide startup validation for the worker binary. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Ritual node executor construction must check that ritual-jq-worker is available and abort startup if the worker binary cannot be resolved or found.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start the node with RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN pointing to a missing path and assert startup fails; point it to a valid ritual-jq-worker binary and assert startup proceeds.
summary: Node startup requires the JQ worker binary.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Start nodebuilder with RITUAL_JQ_WORKER_BIN pointing to a missing path and assert startup fails.
title: Node startup requires JQ worker
